theme: Rebuilt the data pipeline orchestration platform from the ground up — migrating to durable execution, adding observability, and making the system resilient to failures at scale.
highlights: Migrated orchestration layer from Celery to Temporal, eliminating silent task failures and adding durable execution with automatic retry and full execution history across all 12 production pipeline DAGs (3,420 lines added, 47 files changed)
Introduced a formal plugin system using a StagePlugin ABC and entry_points discovery, ending the pattern of teams forking the repo to add custom stages and enabling safe external extensibility
Rewrote schema validation using Pydantic v2, improving validation speed 6x (40ms → 6ms per record) and surfacing 3 previously silenced schema mismatches
Added OpenTelemetry distributed tracing across all stage boundaries, enabling end-to-end latency attribution — first production trace immediately identified a 3-second bottleneck in the S3 read step
Fixed a production race condition in the concurrent stage executor causing data duplication; used property-based testing with Hypothesis to harden the fix against concurrent edge cases
Shipped checkpointing for long-running pipeline runs (>50GB datasets), reducing failure-recovery time from full reprocessing to under 2 minutes
Built pipeline-cli, a developer tool for local pipeline testing with live progress output and diff-mode; adopted by 8 engineers in the first week
Added SLA tracking and Slack/PagerDuty alerting, catching 2 latency regressions in the first week of deployment
Refactored I/O-bound stages to use async execution with httpx, reducing API-aggregation pipeline duration by 35%
Implemented a dead letter queue backed by SQS with replay support; used to recover 14,000 records lost during a downstream API outage
Migrated the test suite from unittest to pytest, adding 80 parametrized tests and increasing coverage from 54% to 78%
Added pipeline versioning with lazy schema migration, eliminating the need for coordinated stop-the-world deploys on schema changes
Shipped cost attribution tracking per pipeline and team, revealing that one misconfigured pipeline consumed 23% of monthly compute budget
Reviewed 8 PRs in this repo, including blocking changes that would have introduced memory leaks and Python 3.12 compatibility issues
impact_statement: pipeline-core is the foundational execution layer for all of dataflow-labs' data processing — every dataset that powers dashboards, ML models, and product features runs through it. The year's work transformed it from a fragile Celery-based system with poor observability into a durable, observable, and extensible platform that can recover from failures, attribute costs, and be extended safely by teams outside the data platform.
collaboration_notes: Reviewed 8 pull requests including blocking changes on the Kafka connector and Python 3.12 migration. The plugin system was designed in direct response to feedback from 3 teams who were maintaining forks.
